all_cases.description: Creates a linear revision chain and reports semantic history.
Reports independent branch heads and a merge node.
Rejects duplicate revisions and unknown parents atomically.
Applies create_table and add_column operations to a minimal linear fixture.
Renames a table and reverses it through downgrade.
Rejects duplicate columns without mutating schema.
Builds a semantic linear upgrade plan from current to target.
Plans a merge target by including missing branch history.
Stamp changes current revisions without applying schema operations.
Downgrades a reversible linear revision and updates direct schema/current state.
Rejects irreversible downgrade before mutating public state.
Creates pending recovery state and recovers idempotently.
Branch upgrade through merge keeps history, heads, current, schema, plan, and ledger consistent.
Stamp updates version projections and future plans without mutating schema.
Downgrade reverts schema, current, ledger, and next upgrade plan together.
Failed migration exposes no mixed public state and is recoverable.
Merge upgrade from one applied branch does not skip the other branch.
